steps.title: Identify dimensions in terms of width
steps.content: The problem states that the length of each rectangular paving stone is 41 cm greater than the width. Let the width be $w$. Therefore, the length is $w + 41$.

steps.title: Apply area formula for rectangles
steps.content: The area $A$ of a rectangle is given by $A = \text{length} \times \text{width}$. Substitute the expressions for length and width: $$A = w \cdot (w + 41)$$. This matches the structure of one of the answer choices.

steps.title: Match to answer choice
steps.content: The derived equation $A = w(w + 41)$ corresponds directly to choice C. No further verification or back-solving is required for this straightforward algebraic substitution.
